page.title: Documentation
page.subtitle: Short guides for reading core pages. This section explains metrics and terms in plain language so the report logic is clear at first glance.

text.beforeOos: This page evaluates ML model quality across different datasets. Segments
text.afterTrain: show how well the model generalizes to new data. In Technical mode, error matrices and SL model thresholds are displayed.

tooltips.oos: OOS (out-of-sample) means data fully excluded from training. OOS metrics provide an unbiased quality estimate without overfitting.
tooltips.train: Train is the training dataset. Metrics are usually higher than OOS, so Train vs OOS comparison is important for overfitting control.

text.beforeMinMove: This page shows the final daily forecast: direction, trade plan, and risks. Pay attention to

tooltips.minMove: MinMove is the minimum expected price movement within the trading window. A low MinMove means weaker movement potential.
tooltips.slModel: The SL model estimates stop-loss hit risk. High SL risk means entries should be handled more cautiously.

text.beforeAccuracy: This page averages class probabilities and computes quality metrics for Day / Day+Micro / Total layers. Metrics
text.afterLogLoss: help assess how confidently the model predicts outcomes.

tooltips.accuracy: Accuracy is the share of correct predictions (Correct / N). Higher is better.
tooltips.logLoss: LogLoss measures how strongly probabilities support the correct class. Lower is better.

text.beforeDeltaAuc: PFI shows how much model quality drops when a feature is shuffled. The key metric
text.afterDeltaAuc: helps identify which features really influence predictions.

tooltips.deltaAuc: Delta AUC shows how much AUC decreases when a feature is shuffled. Bigger drop means higher feature importance.

modelsPage.title: Models and pipeline
modelsPage.subtitle: Detailed description of model layers, their roles, and technical constraints. Switch mode to see business or technical interpretation.

models-overview.business: The model consists of several layers that refine the forecast step by step. The daily model gives a base class (up/flat/down), the micro model refines direction within flat regimes, and the SL model estimates stop-loss risk and may tighten the final decision.
models-overview.tech: All layers run on the same daily records and use strict time rules (as-of / cutoff). Labels and evaluation follow path-based logic to avoid future-data leakage.

models-daily.business: The daily model is the main signal source. It returns three classes (UP/FLAT/DOWN) and their probabilities on a 24-hour horizon. Those probabilities become the base layer for downstream decisions.
models-daily.tech: The daily model is a 3-class classifier (Down/Flat/Up) with Prob3 output. It builds base probabilities P_day that are passed into aggregation.

models-micro.business: The micro model is active only when the daily model predicts flat. Its role is to refine direction within sideways regimes and capture small opportunities.
models-micro.tech: The micro model is activated only for the FLAT class from the daily model. It outputs micro direction (micro-up/micro-down), forming the Day+Micro layer.

models-sl.business: The SL model estimates bad-day risk (high probability of stop-loss). If risk is high, it lowers directional confidence and can switch the day to a more cautious mode.
models-sl.tech: The SL model outputs SL event probability and a binary high-risk decision. In aggregation it builds the Total layer (Day+Micro+SL), reducing risky directions.

models-pipeline.business: The pipeline handles data assembly, missing-data filtering, and a consistent as-of time cut. This is required for fair and reproducible forecasts.
models-pipeline.tech: The pipeline fixes Train/OOS/Recent boundaries, builds as-of cuts, and excludes invalid days. This ensures unbiased metric evaluation.

testsPage.title: Tests and self-checks
testsPage.subtitle: This section explains which checks protect the model from data leakage, invalid metrics, and pipeline degradation. Short notes for each test block are listed below.

sections.tests-overview: Overview of critical pipeline checks. If these tests fail, the model must not go to production calculations.
sections.tests-sanity: Sanity checks validate data and report invariants: continuity, valid ranges, and absence of NaN/Infinity in probabilities.
sections.tests-leakage: Leakage tests detect future-information leakage in features and labels. This is the key protection against inflated metrics.
sections.tests-perf: Performance checks monitor runtime and memory usage to keep the pipeline stable on the full horizon.
